title: arctotheca calendula
intro: Arctotheca calendula, also known as the Cape marigold, is a flowering plant species native to the Western Cape province in South Africa. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Arctotheca calendula include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate menstrual cramps, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Arctotheca calendula can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
